Can I throw this back on for a minute or two?", a man attempts to throw a delicious steak out of a fucking unopened window. Everyone is different, but in general, as someone who enjoys cooking for others regularly: we don't give a shit if you prefer it cooked differently. We want you to enjoy the food we've put effort into making. And if her boss was pro, she would have asked how you like your steak. Assuming someone prefers rare is stupid. If you don't assume but serve rare anyway, don't be offended when people ask for it differently. All that being said, love me some medium rare.

text: For anyone in a future predicament, steak is perfectly safe to eat almost raw so long as the exterior is cooked and there is no mechanical damage. You could safely eat it raw if you sterilized the exterior with alcohol or blow-torch. This is not the case for ground beef because the grinding process destroys the integrity of the meat and mixes surface contaminants into the meat.

text: This is kind of similar to something that happened to my mother as a child while staying with her megabitch aunt. The aunt gave my mum a plate of liver to eat and went out, sternly telling her to make sure she cleaned her plate. The moment she left my mother (who couldn't eat the liver because she hates it) speared it with her fork, ran to the balcony and threw it as hard as she could. When her aunt returned she asked her if she'd eaten the liver. My mother, who is not one of nature's liars, just looked at her empty plate in silence. Her aunt said 'well what's this then?' and pulled out her handkerchief, inside which was the liver. She'd seen her throw it, found out which garden it was in and asked for it back.
